title: Palliser's American Cottage Homes, Plate 4 (Designs 7 and 8)
creator: Palliser, Palliser & Co.
rightsholder_or_source: Internet Archive (Clatsop County Historical Society); published 1878, public domain by age
research_notes: Combined sheet (multiple floor plans + elevations + section). Engraved 1878 pattern-book plate. Page n10 of scan; bears 'Copyright 1878 by Palliser, Palliser & Co.' which has long expired.
research_features: Dense combined Victorian plate: first- and second-floor plans for several small cottages (Designs 7 and 8), plus front/side/rear elevations and a section. Small workman-style cottages with parlor, living room, kitchen, pantry; room sizes labeled in tiny lettering.
feature_highlights: single 1878 plate carrying two separate cottage designs (7 and 8)
first- and second-floor plans for each design, four plans total
elevations, a section, and a central perspective on the same sheet
graphic scale bar ('SCALE ... OF FEET') at the bottom
open piazza on Design 7 and a porch with entry stoop on Design 8
tiny engraved room labels and dimensions
small workman-style rooms: kitchen, pantry, scullery/china room, chambers
steep Gothic-cottage massing in the rendering
supported_examples: rectangular rooms derived from walls with labels and sizes
raster underlay tracing of the engraved plans
roadmap_examples: multi-floor management (first and second floors for each design)

gap_features.why: the plate dimensions its plans through a graphic scale bar across the bottom, which the editor has no annotation for
Design 7's piazza and Design 8's porch/stoop are open columned spaces, not wall-enclosed rooms
